title: hyperbaena eladioana
intro: Hyperbaena eladioana is a plant species in the family Menispermaceae, native to Mexico and Central America, and is also known as the "Mexican moonseed". This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including antiparasitic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hyperbaena eladioana include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to act as an anthelmintic, removing parasitic worms from the body. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for internal use, or applied topically as a poultice or ointment.
